qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Twig sandbox DoS via unbounded loop in formula validation endpoint
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unbounded Twig template iteration in FormulasController.php/validate-formula endpoint causing DoS. The advisory describes SQL injection in inventory-levels-table-data sort parameters via addOrderBy(). Same package (craftcms/commerce) but different endpoint, different vulnerable code (Formulas service Twig sandbox vs inventory sorting SQL), different vulnerability class (DoS via resource exhaustion vs CWE-89 SQL injection), and different root cause (missing iteration cap vs missing input sanitization in SQL ORDER BY).

qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.title: Unauthenticated webhook processing allows gateway abuse and denial of service
qualifying_vulnerability_assessments.advisory_match_reason: The finding describes unauthenticated access to WebhooksController process-webhook action. The advisory describes SQL injection in inventory-levels-table-data sort parameters via addOrderBy(). Same package (craftcms/commerce) but different endpoint, different vulnerable code (WebhooksController allowAnonymous/CSRF bypass vs inventory sorting SQL concatenation), different vulnerability class (missing authentication vs CWE-89 SQL injection), and different root cause (exposed anonymous endpoint vs unsanitized sort parameters in SQL).

target_advisory_judgement.reason: The advisory (GHSA-pmgj-gmm4-jh6j, CVE-2026-29174) describes SQL injection in Craft Commerce inventory-levels-table-data endpoint where sort[0][direction] and sort[0][sortField] parameters are concatenated into an addOrderBy() clause without sanitization (CWE-89). Neither qualifying finding identifies this vulnerable code or root cause. The 'twig-formula-dos' finding describes Twig sandbox DoS in the formulas validation endpoint of craftcms/commerce. The 'unauth-webhook-abuse' finding describes missing authentication in the WebhooksController. Both target different endpoints, different vulnerable functions, and different vulnerability classes than the advisory-described SQL injection.
evidence: Advisory root cause: SQL injection via sort[0][direction]/sort[0][sortField] concatenated into addOrderBy() in inventory-levels-table-data endpoint
twig-formula-dos: Twig sandbox DoS in FormulasController/validate-formula (same package, different endpoint and root cause)
unauth-webhook-abuse: Missing authentication in WebhooksController/process-webhook (same package, different endpoint and root cause)
